body_txt: The commonly spoofed scene for which everyone remembers the first Mission: Impossible film. Ethan Hunt ( Tom Cruise ) must hack a CIA computer while dangling horizontally from two cables inside a heavily protected vault. The floor is pressure and weight sensitive , to the point that even a single drop of condensation dripping off a forgotten soft drink and onto the floor is enough to set off the alarm. To get into the vault, he has to bypass a laser grid in a ceiling ventilation duct . Believe it or not, the scene was actually meant to be a homage to a similar one in the 1964 film Topkapi (and some other films like 1985's Les Spécialistes had similar setups), and it's now a must whenever there's a spy or Heist Episode in a show. For that matter, it's been paid homage to in several installments of the Mission: Impossible Film Series ; it even gets lampshaded in Mission: Impossible II , with the Big Bad predicting that Hunt will break into the building holding the MacGuffin they both want by some sort of "acrobatic insanity". It was actually done in the original Mission: Impossible television series too: the original show had the episode "Doomsday", where Barney used a cable drop to steal the plutonium from a nuclear warhead that was surrounded by photoelectric sensors and so could only be approached from above; and the 1980s remake had the episode "The Lions", where Grant got lowered into the temple and was suspended above the altar so he could tamper with the eponymous lions. Sometimes a Desperate Object Catch is thrown in for added drama. See also Fast-Roping and The Ground Is Lava .
